Vacation Rental Management in Sevierville, TN

Sevierville is the Smokies value market: newer cabins, lower entry prices, and strong revenue. We help owners turn a newer build into a top-performing rental.

Sevierville, Tennessee

Sevierville rewards owners who run their cabin like a business

If you bought a newer cabin in Sevierville, you bought into one of the smartest value plays in the Smokies. Entry prices here run lower than Gatlinburg or Pigeon Forge, the construction is newer, and the revenue still performs. The catch is that strong fundamentals do not run themselves. A great floor plan and a good location only earn what an operator actually captures, and a brand-new asset managed casually leaves real money on the table every single month.

The competitive picture has also shifted. For years owners could ride a rising tide as new supply flooded the market and demand kept climbing. That tide has flattened. With supply growth cooling and nightly rates holding roughly steady, 2026 is not a year that hands out easy wins. It rewards the operators who price intelligently, keep calendars full in the shoulder seasons, and earn the five-star reviews that move a listing up the rankings. Owners who treat their cabin as a part-time hobby tend to feel that shift first.

That is exactly the gap Haven was built to close. We are a local team in Sevier County, here since 2016, and vacation rental management Sevierville owners actually trust because we know this market block by block. We handle the entire operation under one flat fee so your newer asset performs the way the numbers say it should.

Local market snapshot

What the Sevierville numbers mean for your cabin

$321 Smoky Mountains average nightly rate (ADR)
70% Haven target occupancy vs ~55% market average
~30% Ahead of market on revenue with Haven
2016 Locally owned in the Smokies since

The Smoky Mountains nightly rate (ADR) sits near $321 in 2026 and has held roughly flat year over year. That stability is good news, but it changes the game. When rates are not climbing on their own, your income depends on how many of those nights you actually book and how well each one is priced. This is where the difference between average and excellent management shows up in real dollars.

Across our portfolio, Haven homes average around 70% occupancy while the broader market hovers near 55%. That gap of roughly fifteen points is not luck. It comes from a real revenue manager adjusting rates to local demand, marketing that makes listings stand out, and a guest experience that earns repeat bookings. On revenue, our homes run roughly 30% ahead of the market. For a newer Sevierville cabin with strong bones, capturing that upside is the entire point of hiring a professional team.

Sevierville also carries demand drivers worth pricing around. The outlet shopping district pulls steady year-round traffic, the Tennessee Smokies ballpark fills summer weekends, and the city remains an affordable gateway to the Great Smoky Mountains National Park. The newer large cabins going up here attract groups and family reunions willing to pay for space. With supply growth down to about 1% from roughly 8% the year before, the owners who win in 2026 are the ones running their homes with discipline, not the ones hoping for another easy year.

Sevierville market snapshot. Confirm current per-market ADR against AirDNA / Key Data before publishing.

Occupancy opportunity70% with Haven vs roughly 55% market average

Why owners like itLower entry prices and newer assets that respond well to professional management

The Haven standard

What a professionally managed Sevierville cabin looks like

Full-service management

Everything your cabin needs, handled by one local team

Haven is full-service, so once you hand us the keys you are out of the day-to-day. Everything below is covered under one simple flat fee, with no hidden booking fees, no markups on cleaning, and no nickel-and-diming on the side. You get a single local team accountable for the whole operation and the result on your statement.

Bookings & calendar

We list, sync, and optimize your calendar across Airbnb, Vrbo, Booking.com, and direct, so every open night works hard.

Smart human pricing

A real revenue manager who knows this market adjusts your rates daily around demand, events, and seasonality.

Marketing & listings

Professional photography, written-to-convert listings, and multi-channel distribution that puts your cabin in front of more guests.

Housekeeping & turnovers

Vetted local cleaners, hotel-grade standards, and inspected turnovers between every stay.

24/7 guest communication

We answer guests fast, day or night, so small questions never become bad reviews.

Maintenance & contractors

We coordinate trusted local trades and handle issues before they reach you or your guests.

Direct deposit payouts

Clean monthly owner statements and reliable direct-deposit payouts. No surprises, no nickel-and-diming.

Reviews & reputation

A guest-obsessed experience that earns the five-star reviews that drive future bookings and revenue.

Why Sevierville owners choose Haven

Why Sevierville owners choose Haven

We are local, and in Sevierville that matters more than it sounds. We are not a national call center reading from a script in another time zone. We live and work here in the Smokies, we know how Sevierville demand differs from Gatlinburg or the Wears Valley side, and we can put eyes on your cabin and meet a contractor in person. When a guest has an issue at 11pm, a real person who knows your property answers, and that responsiveness is what turns a good stay into a five-star review.

Our pricing model is built to keep you ahead. One flat fee means our incentives line up with yours: we earn more only when you earn more, not by quietly marking up a cleaning invoice. On top of that, a real revenue manager who follows this market sets your rates, not just an algorithm guessing in the dark. In a flat-ADR year like 2026, that human judgment on shoulder-season pricing and event weekends is often the difference between an average return and a great one on a newer asset.

Finally, we stay smaller on purpose. We manage a select group of homes well rather than thousands poorly, which is how our owners reach roughly 70% occupancy and our listings land in the top 1, 5, and 10 percent of Airbnb listings worldwide, backed by a 4.9-star rating across more than 3,400 five-star reviews. For a Sevierville owner who wants a newer cabin treated like the investment it is, that focus is the whole point.

01

A local team

We are based right here in the Smoky Mountains, not in a call center three time zones away. When something needs a person on-site, we are minutes from your door.

02

One simple flat fee

A single, transparent management fee. No hidden booking fees, no markups on cleaning, no nickel-and-diming. You always know exactly what you pay and what you earn.

03

Smart human pricing

A real revenue manager who knows this market sets your rates, not just an algorithm guessing from afar. That is how Haven owners run ahead of the market.

04

Guest-obsessed

A five-star guest experience is the engine of profit. Happy guests leave five-star reviews, rebook, and refer, which lifts your ranking and your rate.

05

Smaller, so we try harder

We deliberately manage a select group of homes well rather than thousands poorly. Your property gets attention a national manager cannot match.

Regulations & compliance

Sevierville permits, inspections, and taxes, handled for you

Operating a short-term rental in Sevierville comes with specific local requirements, and getting them right is not optional. If your cabin sits within the city limits, you need a Short-Term Rental Operational Permit. The initial application fee is $150, with a $50 fee to renew each year. Properties inside the city also need a city business license, and lodging revenue is subject to the same combined tax structure of roughly 12.75% that applies across Sevier County.

The piece that catches the most owners off guard is the inspection. Before your permit is approved, the Sevierville Fire Department conducts a mandatory life-safety inspection of the property, and that inspection has to be repeated every year to keep your permit active. Smoke detectors, fire extinguishers, egress, and similar items all get checked. Miss it and your cabin cannot legally take bookings, which means lost nights during the exact weeks you want to be earning.

STR Operational Permit

Sevierville requires a Short-Term Rental Operational Permit. The initial application fee is $150 and the annual renewal is $50. Haven files it and keeps it current for you.

Mandatory fire department inspection

A life-safety inspection by the Sevierville Fire Department is required before approval and again every year. We prepare your cabin, schedule the inspection, and make sure it passes the first time.

City business license

Properties within Sevierville city limits need a city business license. We handle registration and renewals as part of full-service management.

Lodging taxes around 12.75%

Sevierville cabins carry the same roughly 12.75% combined lodging tax as the rest of Sevier County. Haven collects and remits it correctly across every booking channel.

An emerging value market

Sevierville offers newer construction and more affordable entry prices with strong revenue performance, which makes it ideal for owners who want professional management to get the most out of a newer asset.

Newer build? Make it perform like one. Sevierville's newer cabins reward sharp pricing and a five-star guest experience. Haven turns a great asset into a great-earning one, and keeps the fire inspection and permit on schedule.

Haven handles all of it. We manage your Short-Term Rental Operational Permit and its annual renewal, schedule and prepare your home for the Sevierville Fire Department life-safety inspection every year, sort out the city business license, and make sure lodging taxes are collected and remitted correctly. You should never have to chase a renewal date or guess whether your cabin is compliant. That is our job, and it is included in your flat fee.

Read our full Sevier County short-term rental regulations guide

This summary is for general guidance and is current as of mid-2026. Rules change. Confirm details with the relevant city or county office, or let Haven handle it for you.

Getting started

Switching to Haven is simple

Getting started with Haven is straightforward, and most owners are surprised how little they have to do. Here is how we take a Sevierville cabin from where it is today to performing at its potential.

We evaluate your property

A local expert reviews your home, your market, and your numbers, then shows you what it can realistically earn under professional management.

We make profitable improvements

We recommend the high-return upgrades, photography, and amenities that move your listing up the rankings and lift your nightly rate.

You start earning more

We take over bookings, pricing, guests, cleaning, and maintenance. You watch the calendar fill and the deposits land, without the day-to-day work.

FAQ

Sevierville vacation rental management, answered

Haven charges one simple flat management fee, and that fee covers the entire operation: bookings, smart human pricing, marketing, housekeeping, 24/7 guest communication, maintenance coordination, and your monthly statements. There are no hidden booking fees, no markups on cleaning, and no surprise line items. We earn more only when you earn more, which keeps our incentives lined up with yours. We will walk you through the exact number for your specific cabin on a quick call.

Yes. If your cabin is within the Sevierville city limits, you need a Short-Term Rental Operational Permit. The initial application fee is $150, and it costs $50 to renew each year. Properties inside the city also need a city business license. Haven manages the entire permit process and tracks every renewal date for you so your cabin never lapses out of compliance.

Sevierville requires a mandatory life-safety inspection by the Fire Department before your short-term rental permit is approved, and that inspection has to be repeated every year afterward. Inspectors check items like smoke detectors, fire extinguishers, and safe exits. If you miss the inspection, your cabin cannot legally accept bookings, so timing matters. Haven schedules each inspection, prepares your home to pass, and keeps the cycle on track year after year so you never lose bookable nights.

Lodging revenue in Sevierville is subject to the same combined tax structure of roughly 12.75% that applies across Sevier County, which blends state and local taxes. These taxes are collected from guests and remitted to the appropriate authorities, not paid out of your pocket. Getting the collection and filing right is essential to staying compliant. Haven handles tax collection and remittance as part of full-service management, so it is one less thing on your plate.

For many owners, yes, and that is the appeal of this market. Sevierville is the value play in the Smokies: newer construction, lower entry prices than Gatlinburg or Pigeon Forge, and revenue that still performs strongly. Demand is driven by outlet shopping, the Tennessee Smokies ballpark, and easy access to the National Park, with newer large cabins drawing groups and family reunions. The key is professional operation, because in a year of flat rates and slow supply growth, how well a cabin is run decides how well it earns.

Earnings depend on your cabin's size, location, and amenities, so we do not promise a fixed number. What we can point to is performance: Haven homes average around 70% occupancy versus a market average near 55%, and our portfolio runs roughly 30% ahead of the market on revenue. On a newer Sevierville build with good bones, that gap is meaningful. The best way to get a realistic projection for your specific property is to book a call and let us run the numbers.

Switching is simpler than most owners expect, and we do the heavy lifting. We coordinate the transition of your listings, calendar, and any upcoming reservations so guests are never disrupted, and we make sure your permit, inspection, and tax setup are fully in order. Our process is to evaluate your property and market, make profitable improvements, then take over the day-to-day so you start earning more. Most owners are surprised how little they have to do on their end.

Haven is locally owned and based right here in the Smokies, operating in Sevier County since 2016. We are not a national call center managing thousands of homes from another state. We know how Sevierville demand behaves compared to Gatlinburg and the Wears Valley side, we can put eyes on your cabin, and we work with trusted local contractors. That local knowledge is a big part of why our owners outperform the market average.
